title: Specify MeshCore BLE service coexistence with native LICHEN BLE
description: Why this exists: MeshCore compatibility uses Nordic UART Service UUIDs and raw payload frames, while native LICHEN may expose a separate direct BLE app protocol. What needs to be done: define service UUIDs, advertising names, pairing/PIN policy, dispatcher boundaries, and how MeshCore traffic maps onto the native app contract without conflicting with LICHEN BLE. Acceptance criteria: coexistence plan covers NUS UUIDs, LICHEN native UUIDs, security mode, app discovery, and explicit non-RF-interoperability wording.
design:
MeshCore BLE service coexistence plan, recorded 2026-07-01.

Source/current-state baseline:
- MeshCore compatibility requires Nordic UART Service UUIDs exactly: service 6E400001-B5A3-F393-E0A9-E50E24DCCA9E, RX app->device 6E400002-B5A3-F393-E0A9-E50E24DCCA9E, TX device->app 6E400003-B5A3-F393-E0A9-E50E24DCCA9E. BLE values are raw MeshCore command/response frames with no serial length prefix.
- Current native LICHEN BLE UART bridge in lichen/apps/gateway/src/ble_uart.c also uses the NUS UUID triplet, but carries SLIP-framed IPv6 packets.
- Current Meshtastic BLE in lichen/apps/gateway/src/ble_meshtastic.c uses Meshtastic-specific UUIDs, owns advertising/session state, and is Kconfig-mutually-exclusive with native LICHEN BLE because only one first implementation owns advertising and single app session state.
- Existing gateway Kconfig has LORA_LICHEN_MESHTASTIC_BLE depends on !LORA_LICHEN_BLE. MeshCore should follow the same product-mode exclusivity until a shared BLE owner exists.

Primary decision:
- MeshCore BLE compatibility is a separate product mode, not an additional service in the same firmware image by default.
- It must be mutually exclusive with native LICHEN NUS/SLIP because both claim the same NUS UUIDs with incompatible payload semantics.
- It should also be mutually exclusive with Meshtastic BLE for the first implementation unless project-LICHEN-t2hn.22 or a successor shared BLE owner designs multi-service advertising/session composition.
- Native LICHEN over BLE should eventually move away from generic NUS UUIDs to a LICHEN-specific service UUID if simultaneous native BLE and MeshCore-compatible NUS are required. Until then, the generic NUS UUID cannot distinguish SLIP IPv6 from MeshCore command frames safely.

Recommended Kconfig policy:
- Add CONFIG_LORA_LICHEN_MESHCORE_BLE or equivalent.
- depends on BT && BT_PERIPHERAL && LICHEN_MESHCORE_CODEC.
- select LICHEN_MESHCORE_ADAPTER.
- depends on !LORA_LICHEN_BLE because NUS UUIDs conflict.
- depends on !LORA_LICHEN_MESHTASTIC_BLE for first implementation, matching the single advertising/session owner constraint.
- depends on or selects the Zephyr BLE security pieces needed for authenticated pairing/passkey and encrypted GATT access, including SMP/passkey support as appropriate for the target Zephyr version. If a board/controller cannot provide authenticated pairing, MeshCore BLE must be disabled or explicitly marked insecure/test-only.
- depends on BT_MAX_CONN = 1 for first implementation unless a shared owner supports multiple app sessions.
- Help text must say this is local app compatibility only and does not make LICHEN RF packets MeshCore-compatible.

Advertising/app discovery:
- MeshCore mode should advertise the NUS service UUID in advertising data.
- The default advertised name MUST use a prefix known to be discovered by MeshCore clients. Use MeshCore-LICHEN unless project-LICHEN-9c4y.11 proves another target-client-visible prefix. Do not use LICHEN-MC as the default unless smoke evidence shows the target clients discover it. Research found clients scan for MeshCore-ish names such as MeshCore-, Whisper-, WisCore-, Seeed, Lilygo, HT-, LowMesh_MC_, NRF52.
- Do not use MeshCore-compatible name prefixes in native LICHEN or Meshtastic modes; that would cause wrong clients to connect to incompatible payload semantics.
- Native LICHEN BLE continues to advertise CONFIG_BT_DEVICE_NAME with NUS/SLIP until a LICHEN-specific UUID is designed.
- Meshtastic BLE continues to advertise the Meshtastic service UUID and short name LICHEN as current code does.

GATT/service semantics:
- MeshCore BLE service UUIDs: exact NUS triplet above.
- RX characteristic app->device: write and write-without-response if Zephyr/clients support both; accept one complete raw MeshCore inner frame per write. Reject serial/TCP 0x3c/0x3e length headers on BLE.
- RX and TX characteristics must require encrypted authenticated access in production builds, using Zephyr GATT permission bits/security callbacks appropriate to the target version. Plain WRITE/NOTIFY permissions are acceptable only in native_sim/test configurations that are clearly labeled insecure.
- TX characteristic device->app: notify raw MeshCore response/push frames. If a client uses read semantics, define explicit unsupported/no-data behavior in implementation; MeshCore docs expect TX notifications.
- MTU: request/accept MTU around 185 when central asks, but implementation must work with default 20-byte ATT payload by requiring app writes to fit a GATT value or by documenting fragmentation limits. Because MeshCore docs say one protocol frame per BLE write/notification, LICHEN should not invent BLE fragmentation until real clients prove it is needed.
- Max payload: enforce MeshCore codec limits (176 firmware frame max, 172 serial/TCP client limit as applicable); BLE should use the inner frame max and reject oversize values with ATT invalid length/value not allowed.

Pairing/security/PIN policy:
- MeshCore firmware sources require encrypted MITM/PIN behavior. LICHEN MeshCore mode should require BLE encryption with MITM pairing when the board/controller supports it.
- PIN source hierarchy: configured persistent MeshCore compatibility PIN if project policy creates one; otherwise generated random 6-digit PIN at first boot and persisted; fallback static/test PIN only in native_sim/test builds and clearly marked insecure.
- DEVICE_INFO BLE PIN field should report the active MeshCore compatibility PIN only if the target MeshCore client expects this field and security review accepts exposing it over an already paired/encrypted local connection. If not accepted, report zero/placeholder and document pairing out-of-band.
- CMD_SET_DEVICE_PIN (0x25) is accepted only when persistent compatibility PIN storage exists and the new value is zero or a valid 6-digit PIN. Otherwise ERR_UNSUPPORTED; invalid PIN returns ERR_ILLEGAL_ARG.
- Do not reuse LICHEN native trust pins, OSCORE keys, link keys, or provisioning secrets as MeshCore BLE PINs.

Dispatcher/session boundaries:
- BLE transport owns connection state, RX/TX bounded queues, CCC/notification state, and session epoch.
- MeshCore adapter owns raw command dispatch, response encoding, offline message queue semantics, and command error policy.
- Shared app-interface/identity providers own message send/receive and public identity data. BLE transport must not call LICHEN RF/link APIs directly.
- On disconnect, reset partial RX state, queued writes tied to the session epoch, notification enablement, and any pending app command iterator state. Do not clear persistent compatibility contacts/channels just because BLE disconnects.

Coexistence modes:
- Native LICHEN BLE mode: NUS UUID + SLIP IPv6. Not MeshCore-compatible. Do not advertise MeshCore name.
- MeshCore BLE mode: NUS UUID + raw MeshCore companion frames. Not LICHEN native SLIP. Do not advertise native LCI semantics on this service.
- Meshtastic BLE mode: Meshtastic UUIDs + protobuf ToRadio/FromRadio. Not MeshCore and not native SLIP.
- Future multi-service mode: requires shared BLE advertising/service owner, distinct UUIDs for every semantic surface, explicit BT_MAX_CONN/session policy, and tests that wrong clients cannot bind to wrong payload semantics.

Non-RF-interoperability wording for implementation/docs:
- MeshCore BLE compatibility lets a MeshCore app talk to the local LICHEN node over BLE only.
- It does not let the device join or relay a MeshCore mesh.
- MeshCore raw packet, flood, path, repeater, channel secret, and RF admin commands remain blocked by the command matrix unless explicitly mapped to LICHEN-local behavior.

Downstream requirements:
- project-LICHEN-9c4y.4 should implement MeshCore BLE transport as a separate Kconfig product mode with NUS UUIDs and raw MeshCore frames, production encrypted authenticated GATT permissions, authenticated pairing/passkey policy, and MeshCore PIN storage/validation boundaries.
- project-LICHEN-t2hn.22 should be expanded or related to cover shared BLE advertising/session ownership across native, Meshtastic, and MeshCore modes.
- project-LICHEN-h7t5.7.6.2/native BLE service design should choose LICHEN-specific UUIDs if simultaneous native BLE and MeshCore-compatible NUS is desired.
- project-LICHEN-9c4y.11 smoke tests must include real-client BLE pairing, MTU/write/notify behavior, advertised name using MeshCore-LICHEN or another proven prefix, and wrong-mode failure symptoms.
notes:
Input from project-LICHEN-9c4y.1 research: MeshCore BLE compatibility requires the exact NUS UUID triplet and raw binary values with no serial length prefix. Firmware sources require encrypted MITM/PIN behavior on BLE; app implementations request MTU 185 where available and scan for MeshCore-ish advertising names. Coexistence design must decide whether LICHEN advertises MeshCore-compatible names, how BLE PIN is generated/stored/exposed, and how this NUS service coexists with native LICHEN and Meshtastic compatibility services.
Review evidence 2026-07-01: initial reviewers found two handoff gaps: MeshCore BLE security/PIN requirements were not explicit enough in project-LICHEN-9c4y.4, and LICHEN-MC was an unsafe default advertised name because it was not a known MeshCore discovery prefix. Updated this design to require Zephyr authenticated pairing/passkey/encrypted GATT access, test-only insecure exceptions, MeshCore-LICHEN default name unless smoke evidence proves an alternative, and downstream implementation/smoke-test notes. Follow-up reviewers Carver the 2nd and Herschel the 2nd reported no remaining P0/P1/P2 blockers. No source tests required because this is a Beads-only design task.
close_reason: Completed: MeshCore BLE coexistence design recorded with exact NUS UUIDs, native NUS/SLIP conflict and product-mode exclusivity, MeshCore-LICHEN discovery default, authenticated BLE security/PIN policy, dispatcher/session boundaries, non-RF interoperability wording, and downstream implementation/test Beads updated. Review evidence: two initial reviewers found handoff gaps; fixes applied; two follow-up reviewers reported clean/no P0-P2 blockers. Tests: no source tests required for Beads-only design update.
